title: sciaphila wariana
parts: Sciaphila wariana has many medicinal parts, such as roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of Sciaphila wariana are known to contain compounds like flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the plant are reported to contain terpenoids and phenolic compounds, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of Sciaphila wariana are said to contain sesquiterpenes and sterols, which may exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties.
side_effects: Sciaphila wariana can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. In rare cases, consuming the plant can cause dizziness, nausea, and even vomiting due to its toxic properties. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may also lead to respiratory problems, such as coughing and shortness of breath. If not handled correctly, Sciaphila wariana can cause severe skin burns and blisters.
